  • Purchase of the print or Kindle book includes a free PDF eBook

Who this book is for

This book is for all network engineering roles such as network analysts, administrators, architects, security personnel, support staff, and managers working in both on-premises and cloud environments who are tasked with implementing or using network monitoring solutions. Basic programming knowledge in Python and Go, familiarity with networking concepts, and a fundamental understanding of Docker containers for lab scenarios will be required.

networkautomationcookbook.description_text:
Key Features

  • Design, build, and automate network devices and cloud networking resources using Ansible
  • Orchestrate workflows with AWX and integrate Nautobot as a Source of Truth
  • Start with Event-Driven Ansible and manage Terraform workflows seamlessly
  • Purchase of the print or Kindle book includes a free PDF eBook

Who this book is for

This second edition will help network engineers, DevOps pros, cloud architects, automation engineers, and NREs understand Ansible's role in network automation and how it integrates with tools like Terraform and event-driven architectures. The book requires basic networking knowledge and familiarity with YAML to maximize learning.

masteringpythonnetworking_fourthedition.description_text:
Key Features

  • Leverage Python's powerful libraries and frameworks to build production-ready network automation solutions efficiently and effectively
  • Implement network security, monitoring, and management solutions using modern DevOps tools and cloud-native approaches
  • Utilize AsyncIO and cloud capabilities in Python 3 for AWS and Azure network solutions

Who this book is for

Mastering Python Networking, Fourth edition is for network engineers, developers, and SREs who want to learn Python for network automation, programmability, monitoring, cloud, and data analysis. Network engineers who want to transition from manual to automation-based networks using the latest DevOps tools will also get a lot of useful information from this book.

Basic familiarity with Python programming and networking-related concepts such as Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) will be helpful in getting the most out of this book.

preventcancerfoundation.description_text:
Donations to support the Prevent Cancer Foundation are made to the PayPal Giving Fund, subject to its policies.

Founded in 1985, the Prevent Cancer Foundation has been a leader in cancer prevention and early detection.  As the only nonprofit in the United States solely focused on cancer prevention and early detection, the Foundation has been referred to as "the candle that ignited a bonfire" in the field of prevention.  Investing more than $138 million over the past 29 years, the Prevent Cancer Foundation has always focused its resources on cancers that can be prevented through healthy lifestyle choices or early detection.

Each year, more than 1.6 million Americans are diagnosed with cancer and more than 500,000 will die from the disease.  However, research shows that up to 60 percent of cancer cases and more than 50 percent of cancer deaths are preventable - with the knowledge we have today.
The Foundation has spent the last three decades working for:
  • Reducing the cancer mortality rate by focusing on individual behaviors, engaging in public policy discussions and funding on prevention and early detection research.
  • Raising awareness of all preventable cancers across all populations.
  • Making prevention knowledge and early detection services more accessible for people in medically underserved communities.
  • Collaborating with partners to maximize the effectiveness and reach of the prevention and early detection programs and message.

from_bundle.marketing_blurb: Get AI Networking Cookbook, Modern Network Observability, Linux for Networking Professionals, and more!
from_bundle.detailed_marketing_blurb: Learn the skills and gain the knowledge you need to succeed in system administration and development operations for Linux with this bundle of insightful courses by Packt. Inside this amazing curriculum, you’ll get expert insights on mastering AI networking, Windows 365, PowerShell scripting, and more. Deepen your understanding of SysAdmin and DevOps whether you’re a novice or a seasoned professional with courses like The Ultimate Linux Shell Scripting Guide, Microsoft Intune Cookbook - 2E and help support the Prevent Cancer Foundation with your purchase!
from_bundle.author: Packt

basic_data.human_name: Humble Tech Book Bundle: Data Visualization & Storytelling by Wiley
basic_data.detailed_marketing_blurb: Show—don’t tell—when you integrate storytelling into your data to help paint a broader picture. Unlock eBooks from leading authors in the space, like the For Dummies team, and awesome titles like Cool Infographics: Effective Communication with Data Visualization and Design, Data Points: Visualization That Means Something, and Data-Driven Security: Analysis, Visualization and Dashboards. Pay what you want for a powerful collection of data visualization and storytelling eBooks from some of the biggest names in tech, and help support the Electronic Frontier Foundation!
basic_data.description:
Your data tells a story. Show—don’t tell—when you integrate storytelling into your data to help paint a broader picture. Unlock eBooks from leading authors in the space, like the For Dummies team, and awesome titles like Cool Infographics: Effective Communication with Data Visualization and Design, Data Points: Visualization That Means Something, and Data-Driven Security: Analysis, Visualization and Dashboards. Pay what you want for a powerful collection of data visualization and storytelling eBooks from some of the biggest names in tech, and help support the Electronic Frontier Foundation!

Pay $5 or more. Normally, the total cost for the ebooks in this bundle is as much as $854. Here at Humble Bundle, you choose the price and increase your contribution to upgrade your bundle! This bundle has a minimum $5 purchase.

Read them anywhere. The ebooks in this bundle are available in PDF and EPub formats, so they work on your computer, e-readers, iPads, cell phones, and a wide array of mobile devices! Instructions and a list of recommended reading programs can be found here.

Support charity. Choose where the money goes - between the publisher and to support Electronic Frontier Foundation. If you like what we do, you can leave us a Humble Tip too!

beginningpowerbiforbusinessusers_learnin.human_name: Beginning Power BI for Business Users: Learning to Turn Data into Insights
beginningpowerbiforbusinessusers_learnin.description_text: Beginning Power BI for Business Users: Learning to Turn Data into Insights covers a broad overview of how Power BI fits into the world of data and analytics. Author Paul Fuller shares his expertise, guiding you through each of the specific areas of using Power BI, including:  Data ingestion Modeling Orchestration Visualization Introduction to the DAX language Power BI is an interactive data visualization software product developed by Microsoft with a primary focus on business intelligence. Power BI is a collection of software services, apps, and connectors that work together to turn unrelated sources of data into coherent, visually immersive, and interactive insights. It is part of the Microsoft Power Platform.If you have no experience with reporting or data analytics tools, this book serves as a primer to understanding data analysis. General principles introduced in this book are time-tested and apply broadly while new Power BI features are introduced. If you have experience in other analytical tools, this book serves as a helpful reference book for learning a new tool. Examples cover utilizing common, publicly available data sources such as Excel files, relational databases, and web API data. Edge cases of using features like streaming data, built-in AI, and premium features are also included either as Advanced Topics chapters or Appendices. The author provides sample code you can use to work through the examples he shows to illustrate concepts.

datavisualizationwithexceldashboardsandr.human_name: Data Visualization with Excel Dashboards and Reports
datavisualizationwithexceldashboardsandr.description_text: Large corporations like IBM and Oracle are using Excel dashboards and reports as a Business Intelligence tool, and many other smaller businesses are looking to these tools in order to cut costs for budgetary reasons. An effective analyst not only has to have the technical skills to use Excel in a productive manner but must be able to synthesize data into a story, and then present that story in the most impactful way. Microsoft shows its recognition of this with Excel.  In Excel, there is a major focus on business intelligence and visualization.  Data Visualization with Excel Dashboards and Reports fills the gap between handling data and synthesizing data into meaningful reports.  This title will show readers how to think about their data in ways other than columns and rows. Most Excel books do a nice job discussing the individual functions and tools that can be used to create an "Excel Report".  Titles on Excel charts, Excel pivot tables, and other books that focus on "Tips and Tricks" are useful in their own right; however they don't hit the mark for most data analysts.  The primary reason these titles miss the mark is they are too focused on the mechanical aspects of building a chart, creating a pivot table, or other functionality.  They don't offer these topics in the broader picture by showing how to present and report data in the most effective way. What are the most meaningful ways to show trending?  How do you show relationships in data?  When is showing variances more valuable than showing actual data values?   How do you deal with outliers?  How do you bucket data in the most meaningful way?  How do you show impossible amounts of data without inundating your audience?  In Data Visualization with Excel Reports and Dashboards, readers will get answers to all of these questions. Part technical manual, part analytical guidebook; this title will help Excel users go from reporting data with simple tables full of dull numbers, to creating hi-impact reports and dashboards that will wow management both visually and substantively. This book offers a comprehensive review of a wide array of technical and analytical concepts that will help users create meaningful reports and dashboards. After reading this book, the reader will be able to: Analyze large amounts of data and report their data in a meaningful way Get better visibility into data from different perspectives Quickly slice data into various views on the fly Automate redundant reporting and analyses Create impressive dashboards and What-If analyses Understand the fundamentals of effective visualization Visualize performance comparisons Visualize changes and trends over time

dataanalyticsintheawscloud_buildingadata.human_name: Data Analytics in the AWS Cloud: Building a Data Platform for BI and Predictive Analytics on AWS
dataanalyticsintheawscloud_buildingadata.description_text: Data Analytics in the AWS Cloud: Using AWS Data Lakes and Warehouses for BI Reporting and Predictive Analytics helps you learn how to store, process, and analyze data on the AWS Cloud platform. The book focuses on data analytics, from data engineering to data analysis to data science, including CI/CD, Devops/ MLOps and Business Intelligence. The scope of the book covers all the aspects of data pipelines, from data acquisition, import and storage, to visualization and reporting, with a particular emphasis on serverless data engineering, which reduces overheads and costs, and improves stability and maintainability. Chapters include: Chapter 1: AWS Data Lakes and Analytics Chapter 2: Setting up a Data Analytics Team Chapter 3: Storage Chapter 4: Managing the Lake Chapter 5: Populating the Lake Chapter 6: Processing and Preparation Chapter 7: Analyzing the Lake Chapter 8: Machine Learning at Scale Chapter 9: Visualization Chapter 10: Example Architectures The book helps you understand the integration of machine learning predictions with analytics engines and visualization tools, provides examples of architectures from real-use cases, and shows you advanced uses of Amazon Athena, a service that is central to the modern AWS data engineering workflow.

presentbeyondmeasure_designvisualizeandd.human_name: Present Beyond Measure: Design, Visualize, and Deliver Data Stories That Inspire Action
presentbeyondmeasure_designvisualizeandd.description_text: Billions of dollars and thousands of hours are lost every year during ineffective business meetings worldwide. Data practitioners painstakingly present their valuable insights, only to fall flat, inspiring more yawn than yes! Present Beyond Measure provides a 4-phase, step-by-step blueprint for planning, designing, visualizing, and delivering compelling data presentations. Learn how to use neuroscience and cinematic storytelling techniques to galvanize your stakeholders into action. By the final page, youll know exactly how to: -          Choose the data that matters most to your decision-makers -          Speak to different audience personality types (even the most challenging) -          Infuse your presentation with a persuasive narrative storyline -          Craft recommendations that get approved and activated -          Design simple, stunning slides that communicate without confusing -          Transmit your data story with best-practice visualization techniques -          Prepare for and deliver your presentation like a professional speaker

functionalaestheticsfordatavisualization.description_text: This is a visually engaging book with a blend of interdisciplinary ideas for creating data-created interfaces, such as interactive dashboards and visualizations. We refer to this collection of principles as Functional Aesthetics based on cognitive psychology, design, information retrieval, and visualization to explore ways in which information can be presented to humans that is both meaningful and delightful. Supported heavily by practical examples and research, the book showcases how functional aesthetics makes data easier to see and understand. Key topics include: Visual perception including color, movement, and design The importance of semantics and how they are employed as a way to express meaning in visual artifacts Data visualization linguistics The influence of culture and language on semantics What is the intent for seeing and understanding a dataset and how is that intent expressed visually and analytically Bringing together all of the pieces to work with chart sizing for specific views, layout with consideration for order  and reading patterns, movement and interaction, visual flow Innovation and whats next in AI and smart interfaces

handsonwithgoogledatastudio_adatacitizen.human_name: Hands On With Google Data Studio: A Data Citizen's Survival Guide
handsonwithgoogledatastudio_adatacitizen.description_text: With more and more data in our lives, we need new ways to take control of that data and make sense of it in our professional, business, and community lives. Learning Google Data Studio can help you turn your data into useful information by providing an insight that you can then easily discuss and share with others. Data is now available more than ever for anyone with a computer and an internet connection to access. Even if you are miles away from being a data scientist, chances are good that you will want to, or need to pick up the ability to do data visualization.What does this mean? Well, the good news is that you now have free tools that let you access and visualize data in the same ways that large businesses and organizations do. You no longer have to wait for someone else to build an application that shows common business trends you may have missed otherwise. You no longer have to wait for someone in accouting to turn your budget data into a report. Small business owners are not at the mercy of a marketing partner to tell them how their websites or business listings are doing. Young professionals can now gather tons of public data, so they can then come to their own conclusions and share their insights with others. All you need now is a Gmail account, some curiosity and a guide to get you started! Let's build your first data set; you will learn how to navigate the Data Studio Main Interface to build on a blank canvas, learn to add style by changing your color palette, add a banner or a logo and more! You will be excited share this report with all of your colleauges! In this book, you will learn how to use Google Data Studio to access data all the way from the personal to public. Youll be able to turn that data into beautiful, interactive information all while learning the fundamentals of data visualization. You will then be able to share this information privately with individuals or even the whole world! We will take you step-by-step through practical and real life examples while sharing expert tips along the way. Get ready to take control of your data today by becoming a data citizen wiz!

coolinfographics.human_name: Cool Infographics: Effective Communication with Data Visualization and Design
coolinfographics.description_text: The book will cover the most effective uses of data visualization and infographic design to communicate your message.  Online infographics are used for marketing purposes and are the most visible and recognized category of infographics.  However, businesses are also using infographics to visualize budgets, marketing strategies, business plans, sales presentations, Board Meeting presentations, annual reports, consumer research statistics and as visual explanations of their products and services to customers. Even individuals are now using infographics to create unique and outstanding resumes. Utilizing many visual examples and links to online sources, the major topics would include: Why infographics work Online, marketing infographics Internal, confidential infographics Better presentations with infographics and data visualization How to create an infographic resume Designing infographics tutorial

graphanalysisandvisualization.human_name: Graph Analysis and Visualization: Discovering Business Opportunity in Linked Data
graphanalysisandvisualization.description_text: The book describes how to use graph analytics and visualization techniques to generate novel insights from a variety of data sources, including Big Data.  The book takes users through concrete examples drawn from the worlds of business, sports, finance, security and social media to go from data to insights and communication. It contains real-world examples of how networks are being used in some of the biggest tech companies,  in sports, and on Wall Street (among others). The book will also have the added benefit of showing how to create beautiful visualizations that communicate discovered patterns and connections that decision-makers can exploit. The book will include: 1. Graphical examples of the networks using clear and insightful visualizations. 2. Clear, engaging and easy to use data sets that have been curated for the purpose of network analysis. e.g. predicting pitches for MLB teams; targeting business acquisitions, and more. 3. Examples of different software tools and programming languages you can use to extract insight from the data e.g. Gephi, NodeXL, D3. 4.  Code examples using the popular Python programming language.   All of the code examples and related data sets will be provided on the book's companion website.

thevisualorganization.human_name: The Visual Organization: Data Visualization, Big Data, and the Quest for Better Decisions
thevisualorganization.description_text: You need not be a data scientist to present data well. Professionals cannay, mustrepresent their data in a persuasive visual format. This book will help them do just that.  The book will walk the reader through the current landscape of options available for data visualization tools, such as Tableau, Ease.ly, and others.  It will discuss common mistakes people make when presenting data and include in depth real-world examples to show readers what groundbreaking data visualization looks like and the impact it can have.

dataheroplaybook_developingyourdataleade.human_name: The Data Hero Playbook: Developing Your Data Leadership Superpowers
dataheroplaybook_developingyourdataleade.description_text: Are you a data leader tasked to promote a culture of data-enabled decision making within your company? Are you passionate about helping your company, or your clients, to realize the transformative power of data? If yes, then your timing couldnt be any better - and youve come to the right place. This book is dedicated to sharing insights on the behaviors, and more importantly the mindset, that data leaders must adopt to succeed in an era of constant change and AI-fueled disruption. The rise of the digital age has brought the critical importance, and potential value, of data to the forefront for most senior business executives. The idea of data as the new oil has spread like wildfire over the last decade, fueled by the explosive growth of Netflix, Uber, and other digital natives that have built successful businesses around their ability to leverage data for competitive advantage. A desire to extract value from data and digitally transform led many companies to hire Chief Data Officers (CDOs) and significantly invest in data and analytics functions. The more recent explosion of AI has only intensified this focus on data, and the expectations for CDOs and their teams are high. This focus on data puts the hard-working professionals tasked with managing and optimizing data within large companies in a unique and potentially lucrative position. The process used to extract raw data and refine it into valuable business insights requires a combination of specialized skills found only in data and analytics functions. This is particularly the case with Artificial Intelligence, which puts everyone in the data and AI value chain at a distinct advantage in terms of their individual growth opportunities. As data can transform businesses, it can also transform careers. Upon conversations with many business professionals, Hawker discovered that despite significant investments in AI and data, companies were not realizing its full potential. The root of the problem is a negative mindset of those people and companies engaged in the practice of managing data. This mindset promotes a culture in data teams that reinforces the status quo and stunts any meaningful opportunities for to embrace change. It is the biggest reason why so many companies see the incredible potential from data yet fail to realize its promise. This negative mindset provides fertile ground for the creation of a dysfunctional relationship between the producers and consumers of data, and it disempowers data leaders from innovating and influencing any meaningful changes to their organizational cultures. It reinforces an inward focus that puts more value on the management of data than customer satisfaction, and in doing so it inverts the entire data value proposition. Empowering data leaders to do away with these old ways of thinking in favor of new, more productive perspectives, is the core theme of the book. Hawker calls this new way of thinking Heroic Data Leadership, an approach that jettisons the negative mindsets which no longer serve data professionals, and wildly embraces those which take a more positive view of their role, their customers, and their data. In doing so, this new mindset will enable hard-working and well-intentioned leaders to become the data heroes their organizations so desperately need, and it will finally allow organizations to realize the full potential of their data. The Data Hero Playbook includes: Specific recommendations of behaviors needed to embrace more positive mindsets Quotes and anecdotes from people running a data function at large companies today Fictional stories / narratives describing the transition of data practitioners from negative mindsets (current state), to more positive mindsets (future states). Ultimately, theres never been a better time to be in a data career, and Hawker helps leaders find their ideal path.

bigbookofdashboards_visualizingyourdatau.human_name: The Big Book of Dashboards: Visualizing Your Data Using Real-World Business Scenarios
bigbookofdashboards_visualizingyourdatau.description_text: In The Dashboard Book, the authors will lay out a variety of examples of successful dashboards so that the reader can find a scenario that closely matches what he or she is tasked with visualizing. Anyone charged with building or overseeing the development of business dashboards, from entry level data analysts and line managers to CTOs, CMOs, and CEOs, will learn how to better visualize their data by consulting the various examples in this book. Topics covered include: Pre-attentive attributes and how this relates to chart and dashboard design Thorough discussion and illustration of when and how to use specific charts Numerous examples of good dashboards with discussion of what works and what does not The benefits and challenges of interactivity in data visualizations which will be shown through various screen shots Discussion of when clients or business superiors demand bad design elements and how to address this challenge to minimize negative impact In addition to the variety of real-world examples, The Dashboard Book will also feature advice from other experts in the Data Viz field like Cole Nussbaumer (Storytellingwithdata.com) and Kelly Martin (VizCandy.ca).

developers.developer-name: Steve Wexler, Jeffrey Shaffer, Andy Cotgreave

teachyourselfvisuallypowerbi.description_text: The visual learners guide to turning data into visualizing graphics and charts! Teach Yourself Visually Power Bi is your ultimate guide to turning your hard to read and interpret data into visual charts you can actually understand! Alexander Loth delivers a start-to-finish guide to applying the Power BI platform to your own firms data. Youll discover how to start exploring your data sources, build data models, visualize your results, and create compelling reports that motivate decisive action.  Tackle the basics of Microsoft Power BI and, when youre done with that, move on to advanced functions like accessing data with DAX and app integrations Guide your organizations direction and decisions with rock-solid conclusions based on real-world data Impress your bosses and confidently lead your direct reports with exciting insights drawn from Power BIs useful visualization tools

theartofinsight_howgreatvisualizationdes.human_name: The Art of Insight: How Great Visualization Designers Think
theartofinsight_howgreatvisualizationdes.description_text: The Art of Insight: How Great Visualization Designers Think is a book about making design decisions in difficult situations. Decision-making is an essential skill for designers because anyone can create a data visualization with just a few clicks. Data is easily available online, and multiple free and easy-to-use software tools have appeared in the past few years. These developments have led to an explosion in the amount and variety of graphs, charts, and maps. We see them everywhere, from news publications to social media. Cairo explains this is a positive phenomenon, but only if the creators of those visualizations are able to think clearly and ethically about what they are doing. As the famous line from the 2002 Spider-Man movie says, with great power comes great responsibility. Visualization books often focus on rules for creating charts and maps, but rarely explain the origin of those rules. Readers are told to start all graphs at a zero baseline, never use pie charts, maximize the data-ink ratio, and so on. Cairo argues that this approach is misguided: it shoehorns designers into a single rigid mode of thinking, based only on the perspective of the book's author or authors.   Cairo's approach in how great visualization designers think is different. He helps every reader develop their own thinking and decision-making framework. First, they explore the questions a designer should ask when creating a visualization. Designers should know what they are visualizing and why that dataset needs to be visualized. Designers should also think about how to most effectively convey information based on who might be reading their graphics. He also illustrates how great visualization designers answer these questions by analyzing visualizations from designers all around the world. His sample of cases and designers is the most diverse ever in any visualization book, drawing visualizations not only from the English-speaking world, but also from countries such as China, Korea, India, Egypt, and Ukraine, and from designers with a wide diversity of backgrounds. These case analyses will reveal how individuals with great influence in the field and/or a substantial body of work think: what decisions they had to make and how they justify those decisions, how they shaped their data and visuals, how they styled their graphics, and how they structured their data narratives. We humans learn by copying and doing. Cairo expects readers of How Great Visualization Designers Think to borrow ideas from these designers, be inspired by them, and apply what they learned from the book to their own work.   Cairo wants readers to understand that their decisions should be based on clear thinking and reasoning based on facts. He describes empirical research, heuristics, and historical conventions, which can help any designer make better decisions. He also wants visual creatives to understand that sometimes the rules of visualization can and should be broken.

datapoints.human_name: Data Points: Visualization That Means Something
datapoints.description_text: The book is a fresh look at visualization as a medium rather than just a tool for analysis. It will be example-driven, featuring work from people in areas of art, design, business, statistics, computer science, cartography, and online media, as well as many of the author's own illustrations. The major sections of the book will cover: Visualization as Medium In the same way not all movies are documentaries, not all visualization is about optimal visual perception. Data Representation There are rules across all visualization applications, such as the use of appropriate shapes to accurately represent values. Design with Purpose Rules can be broken though. It all depends on who and what youre designing for.

tableaufordummies2ndedition.description_text: Put your data into action with TableauThere's no shortage of data in the business world. There is, however, a shortage of people who know how to weave data into a story that explains a business in a way that is understandable by everyone. Tableau gives you the power to understand your data and put it in a format that is appealing and meaningful for everyone who needs to see it. All that's missing is a guide that explains how to get from data to brilliance. This book fills that gap by offering insight from an enterprise data pro on how to transform data into a clear and memorable visual presentation.Inside... Navigate the user interface Connect to spreadsheets, databases, and other sources of data  Use the drag-and-drop features to create visualizations Work with templates and sample workbooks Add graphs, charts, and other information Create dashboards from multiple sources of data Export your visualizations to multiple formats

dataanalyticsandvisualizationall-in-onef.human_name: Data Analytics & Visualization All-in-One For Dummies
dataanalyticsandvisualizationall-in-onef.description_text: Your first stop in becoming a data analytics pro It takes a lot of tech know-how to become a successful data analyst. Not only do you need to understand how data sources like data lakes work, you need to also know how to extract data using a tool like Microsoft Power BI, wrangle that data in a tool like Microsoft Excel, and present the data in a way that makes sense using a tool like Tableau. The super-charged analysts even know next-level tech like Python, R, and SPSS to get the job done. Sound daunting? Relax - this book has you covered, explaining the essentials of becoming a priceless data jockey who knows how to answer questions with data. Inside... Mining data from the source Organizing and analyzing data in Excel Getting your point across with Tableau Expanding your know-how with Python and R Turning data into conclusions

developers.developer-name: Jack A. Hyman, Luca Massaron, Paul McFedries, John Paul Mueller, Jonathan Reichental, Joseph Schmuller, Alan R. Simon, Allen G. Taylor

data-drivensecurity_analysisvisualizatio.human_name: Data-Driven Security: Analysis, Visualization and Dashboards
data-drivensecurity_analysisvisualizatio.description_text: Everything in the book will have practical application for information security professionals. The entire purpose of data analysis and visualization is to gather feedback from the environment to make better and more informed technology decisions. Within information security that means identifying ways to prevent or detect breaches and then measuring the effectiveness in doing so, which is all wrapped up under "risk management".  All of the examples will be directed at answering real-world questions.  One of the key points is not just to analyze what is in front of us, but collect and analyze the data we need to answer the questions that will lead to better decisions and prevention of hacks and vulnerabilities. The book will present the core elements of analyzing I.T. system data and information security feedback by using 30 use cases and domain-specific data sets with a focus on practical how-to. This hands-on approach will be covered in context and will not be limited to just the analysis, but all the supporting skills needed to learn from our data.  Data analysis from start to finish: from the data collection and preparation through the data storage and management fundamentals then into the analysis and finally data visualization and communication techniques all in the context of security. Use cases will include: Discovering anomalous firewall traffic How to acquire and prepare security data Creating a repeatable data analysis toolkit and workflow Whitehat stats report Security event correlation Vulnerability counts Using inferential stats to detect malware outbreaks Visualizing system logs Mapping Botnets Using NLP and Data Loss Prevention Predicting rogue behavior How to perform predictive analytics

microsoftpowerbifordummies2ndedition.human_name: Microsoft Power BI For Dummies, 2nd Edition
microsoftpowerbifordummies2ndedition.description_text: Make smarter business decisions with Microsoft PowerBI Microsoft PowerBI For Dummies, 2nd Edition provides the foundations for using the platform to manage and understand your data. From visualizing, mapping and interpreting, PowerBI has what you need to make smart business decisions. Once you have your data ready, craft and deploy your data models and create reports. Then, you can dig into the PowerBI programming language DAX, no matter if you're a programming pro or a newbie. Find out how to make sense of your data with Microsoft PowerBI FD 2e. Updates: Implemented AI and copilot into its programs Enhanced customization with visuals Improved collaboration and sharing Work on reports together in real time, use commenting tools, and track changes Revert to previous versions of reports.

eff.description_text:
Donations to support Electronic Frontier Foundation are made to  PayPal Giving Fund, subject to its policies.

The Electronic Frontier Foundation is a non-profit that defends your rights in the digital world. This world-class team of tech-savvy attorneys, activists, and technologists fight in courtrooms and Congress to protect privacy and free expression online and throughout the world.

visualizethis.human_name: Visualize This: The FlowingData Guide to Design, Visualization, and Statistics, 2nd Edition
visualizethis.description_text: A decade ago with his groundbreaking first edition of Visualize This Nath Yau told us that data doesn't decrease - it is ever-increasing and can be overwhelming to organize in a way that makes sense to its intended audience. In the decade since we've seen even more data and visualizations invade every aspect of business, our personal lives, and the public sphere. Nathan Yau's timely update in Visualize This 2nd edition continues to show the practical benefits of visualizing data in a way we can maximize its potential and tell a story in a clear, concise manner. With this completely updated full-color book, data visualization guru and author Nathan Yau uses step-by-step tutorials to show you how to visualize and tell stories with data. He explains how to gather, parse, and format data and then design high quality graphics that help you explore and present patterns, outliers, and relationships. Presents a unique approach to visualizing and telling stories with data, from a data visualization expert and the creator of flowingdata.com, Nathan Yau with this edition benefiting from another decade of Nathan's insight and experience Offers step-by-step tutorials and practical design tips for creating statistical graphics, geographical maps, and information design to find meaning in the numbers Details tools that can be used to visualize data-native graphics for the Web, with major updates since the first edition for the R packages that so much data visualization depends on now as well as updated coverage of widely used JavaScript libraries Contains numerous examples and descriptions of patterns and outliers and explains how to show them Visualize This 2nd edition demonstrates how to explain data visually so that you can present your information in a way that is easy to understand and appealing.

eff.description_text:
Donations to support Electronic Frontier Foundation are made to  PayPal Giving Fund, subject to its policies.

The Electronic Frontier Foundation is a non-profit that defends your rights in the digital world. This world-class team of tech-savvy attorneys, activists, and technologists fight in courtrooms and Congress to protect privacy and free expression online and throughout the world.

from_bundle.marketing_blurb: Get Tableau For Dummies, 2nd Edition, Teach Yourself VISUALLY Power BI, Data Points: Visualization That Means Something, and more!
from_bundle.detailed_marketing_blurb: Show—don’t tell—when you integrate storytelling into your data to help paint a broader picture. Unlock eBooks from leading authors in the space, like the For Dummies team, and awesome titles like Cool Infographics: Effective Communication with Data Visualization and Design, Data Points: Visualization That Means Something, and Data-Driven Security: Analysis, Visualization and Dashboards. Pay what you want for a powerful collection of data visualization and storytelling eBooks from some of the biggest names in tech, and help support the Electronic Frontier Foundation!
from_bundle.author: Wiley

basic_data.human_name: Humble Video Learning Bundle: The Cybersecurity & AI Defense Mega Bundle by Packt
basic_data.detailed_marketing_blurb: Nothing gets past our latest Humble Video Learning Bundle: The Cybersecurity & AI Defense Mega Bundle! Learn how to protect your digital world using AI defensive tactics with exceptional courses from Packt. Prepare for your new career with video learning courses like Cybersecurity Fundamentals & Certification Prep – ISC2 Pathway, Mastering Cybersecurity with Python - From Basics to Advanced Defense, AZ-500 - Microsoft Azure Security Technologies - A Prep Course, more. Go from zero to cybersecurity hero, and help support the Starlight Foundation with your purchase!
basic_data.short_marketing_blurb: Defend your sensitive data with AI
basic_data.description:
Defend your sensitive data with AI. Nothing gets past our latest Humble Video Learning Bundle: The Cybersecurity & AI Defense Mega Bundle! Learn how to protect your digital world using AI defensive tactics with exceptional courses from Packt. Prepare for your new career with video learning courses like Cybersecurity Fundamentals & Certification Prep – ISC2 Pathway, Mastering Cybersecurity with Python - From Basics to Advanced Defense, AZ-500 - Microsoft Azure Security Technologies - A Prep Course, more. Go from zero to cybersecurity hero, and help support the Starlight Foundation with your purchase!

Pay $3 or more. Normally, the total cost for the software in this bundle is as much as $2,632. Here at Humble Bundle, you choose the price and increase your contribution to upgrade your bundle! This bundle has a minimum $3 purchase.

Redeem your keys. All keys can be redeemed on the Packt website.

Support charity. Choose where the money goes - between the publisher and to support Starlight Foundation. If you like what we do, you can leave us a Humble Tip too!

chatgptforcybersecurity_aitoolsforprotec.human_name: ChatGPT for Cybersecurity: AI Tools for Protection & Threat Mitigation
chatgptforcybersecurity_aitoolsforprotec.description_text: This course equips cybersecurity professionals to integrate ChatGPT into threat analysis, incident response, script automation, and security training. Gain practical skills and hands-on experience using AI tools to strengthen and streamline your cybersecurity workflows.

exploitingandsecuringaiandmcpsystems.description_text: This course teaches you to build, exploit, and secure MCP servers using Python and FastMCP SDK. You’ll learn to identify and exploit common vulnerabilities in MCP systems while building a secure AI infrastructure. Practical labs and step-by-step guidance ensure hands-on learning.

digitalidentityandsecurity_anintroductio.human_name: Digital Identity and Security - An Introduction to PKI (hands-on)
digitalidentityandsecurity_anintroductio.description_text: Delve into the core concepts of digital security, focusing on encryption, PKI, and certificate authorities. Learn how to implement SSL/TLS, manage digital signatures, and explore key tools for securing online transactions and communications.

azurecloudsecuritylab_ethicalhackingandi.human_name: Azure Cloud Security Lab: Ethical Hacking and Initial Access Techniques
azurecloudsecuritylab_ethicalhackingandi.description_text: Dive into ethical hacking for Azure environments, exploring reconnaissance, initial access methods like brute force, phishing, and storage hunting. Learn how to secure and mitigate threats in Azure with hands-on labs and practical tools.

aisecurityfundamentals_llmthreatsandowas.human_name: AI Security Fundamentals - LLM Threats and OWASP Principles 2026
aisecurityfundamentals_llmthreatsandowas.description_text: Learn to secure LLM applications by addressing threats like prompt injection, data poisoning, and implementing OWASP principles for effective protection.

ccnacybersecurity200-201_videotrainingse.description_text: This CCNA Cybersecurity (200-201) training series offers a thorough exploration of cybersecurity principles, attack methods, and best practices. Designed for professionals seeking certification, it covers everything from foundational defense to advanced network traffic analysis. Prepare for the exam with hands-on learning and expert guidance.

az-500_microsoftazuresecuritytechnologie.human_name: AZ-500 - Microsoft Azure Security Technologies - A Prep Course
az-500_microsoftazuresecuritytechnologie.description_text: This in-depth course on Microsoft Azure Security Technologies will fully prepare you for the AZ-500 exam. Explore essential topics like securing Active Directory, Azure Sentinel, networks, databases, and workloads, and gain practical experience with hands-on demonstrations to strengthen your Azure security skills.

developers.developer-name: Mr. Mumshad Mannambeth, Mr. Rithin Skaria

securityplus701_hands-oncybersecuritylab.description_text: This lab-driven cybersecurity course builds practical skills through real attack and defense scenarios. You will work with reconnaissance, exploitation, scripting, and incident response tools while gaining hands-on experience aligned with Security Plus SY0-701 objectives.

masteringcybersecuritywithpython_frombas.human_name: Mastering Cybersecurity with Python - From Basics to Advanced Defense
masteringcybersecuritywithpython_frombas.description_text: This course equips you with the skills to leverage Python for cybersecurity. Dive into coding fundamentals, data structures, and advanced programming concepts. Learn to create secure systems, handle data encryption, and develop authentication tools to fortify digital defenses.

cloudsecurityforitprofessionals.description_text: Strengthen your cloud security knowledge with key concepts such as IAM, data encryption, and network security. This course covers practical techniques for protecting cloud infrastructures and preventing breaches. Perfect for IT professionals looking to advance their security expertise.

comptiasecuritypluscertification_aprepco.human_name: CompTIA Security+ Certification - A Prep Course
comptiasecuritypluscertification_aprepco.description_text: This course will guide you through all the key concepts necessary to pass the CompTIA Security+ certification exam. Learn about security controls, cryptography, network attacks, risk management, and much more. With hands-on projects and demos, you'll gain practical knowledge and be fully prepared for the certification.

absolutebeginnersguidetocybersecurity202.human_name: The Absolute Beginner's Guide to Cyber Security 2026 - Part 1
absolutebeginnersguidetocybersecurity202.description_text: Dive into the basics of cyber security with this introductory course. Understand key concepts like networks, common cyber attacks, defense strategies, and how to stay safe in today’s digital landscape. Perfect for newcomers to the field.

cybersecurityfundamentalsandcertificatio.human_name: Cybersecurity Fundamentals & Certification Prep - ISC2 Pathway
cybersecurityfundamentalsandcertificatio.description_text: This course introduces the core principles of cybersecurity and the frameworks used to protect modern digital environments. It provides a structured learning path covering security foundations, risk management, governance, and incident response. Designed for beginners, it also supports ISC2 CC certification readiness.

securityservicesinaws.description_text: Learn to secure your AWS environments with advanced security tools and strategies. This course covers the foundational concepts of AWS security, including encryption, threat detection, incident response, and recovery solutions. Equip yourself with the knowledge to manage security across multi-account AWS environments.

beginners2025cybersecurityawarenesstrain.human_name: The Beginners 2025 Cyber Security Awareness Training Course
beginners2025cybersecurityawarenesstrain.description_text: This comprehensive course is tailored to equip beginners with a solid understanding of cyber security awareness, social engineering, network security, and online self-defense. Regardless of your prior experience in IT or cyber security, you will gain confidence in securely managing emails, files, mobile phones, computers, and browsing the Internet.

employeecybersecurityawarenessintheageof.human_name: Employee Cybersecurity Awareness in the Age of AI
employeecybersecurityawarenessintheageof.description_text: This course empowers employees to recognize and respond to AI-enhanced cybersecurity threats. Explore AI-driven phishing, social engineering, and data risks. Build essential cyber hygiene habits and collaborate effectively to strengthen your organization’s defenses.

riskmanagementforcybersecuritymanagers.human_name: Risk Management for Cyber Security Managers
riskmanagementforcybersecuritymanagers.description_text: Gain expert knowledge in cybersecurity risk management with this course tailored for professionals. Learn to identify, assess, and manage risks while mastering key practices such as vulnerability management and communicating risks to stakeholders. Enhance your career with proven strategies and practical tools.

aiandcybersecurity_threatsglobalactorsan.human_name: AI & Cybersecurity: Threats, Global Actors, and Trends in 2025
aiandcybersecurity_threatsglobalactorsan.description_text: Dive into the future of cybersecurity, exploring AI-driven threats, global cyber actors, and defense strategies. Understand the evolving landscape of AI-powered malware, state-sponsored cyberattacks, and proactive defense tactics, preparing you for 2025's challenges.

starlightfoundation.description_text:
Donations to support Starlight Foundation are made to  PayPal Giving Fund, subject to its policies.

Starlight Children’s Foundation delivers happiness to seriously ill children and their families. Since 1982, Starlight’s ground-breaking and innovative programs, like Starlight Virtual Reality, Starlight Hospital Wear, and Starlight Gaming, have positively impacted 21 million kids at more than 800 children’s hospitals across the U.S. To learn more, visit www.starlight.org.

cybersecurityessentials_fromzerotosecure.human_name: Cybersecurity Essentials - From Zero to Secure
cybersecurityessentials_fromzerotosecure.description_text: This foundational course covers everything from the basics of cybersecurity to hands-on labs and tools. Designed for beginners, it explains key threats, security frameworks, and safe practices. Whether you're pivoting careers or upskilling, this course gets you cyber-ready.

ai-poweredcyberdefense_automatingsecurit.human_name: AI-Powered Cyber Defense: Automating Security with Intelligent Agents
ai-poweredcyberdefense_automatingsecurit.description_text: Master AI-powered cybersecurity automation with ChatGPT and Microsoft Sentinel, covering threat detection, integration, and more.

comptiasecuritypluscertificationsy0-701_.human_name: CompTIA Security+ Certification (SY0-701): The Total Course
comptiasecuritypluscertificationsy0-701_.description_text: Jumpstart your cybersecurity career with the TOTAL: CompTIA Security+ Certification (SY0-701) course, featuring industry experts Mike Meyers, Dan Lachance, and Lyndon Williams, covering updated exam objectives and practical skills for aspiring cybersecurity professionals.

developers.developer-name: Total Seminars, Lyndon Williams, Dan Lachance

ccstcybersecurityvideotrainingseries.description_text: Comprehensive Cisco CCST Cybersecurity exam training covering threat landscapes, network defense, and incident response. Build the knowledge and skills needed to excel in certification and professional cybersecurity roles.
